service: Amazon RDS Storage and SQL Server
one_liner: Managed relational database service with gp2/gp3 storage tiers and full SQL Server engine support including read replicas.
when_to_use: Need a managed relational database with standard SQL engines (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server)
SQL Server workloads that need read replicas for read scaling
Workloads needing predictable IOPS with gp3 or burstable IOPS with gp2
Applications that need automated backups, patching, and Multi-AZ failover
when_not_to_use: Need global cross-region replication with sub-second RPO (use Aurora Global Database)
Need serverless auto-scaling database (use Aurora Serverless v2)
Key-value or document access patterns at massive scale (use DynamoDB)
Need full OS-level control over the database server (use EC2 self-managed)
key_facts: gp2 storage: baseline of 3 IOPS per GB, minimum 100 IOPS, burst up to 3,000 IOPS for volumes under 1TB. Volumes of 1TB+ get 3,000+ baseline IOPS (no burst needed).
gp3 storage: baseline 3,000 IOPS and 125 MiB/s throughput regardless of volume size. Can provision up to 16,000 IOPS and 1,000 MiB/s independently.
RDS for SQL Server DOES support read replicas (added 2022). Supported for SQL Server 2016 SP2 CU8+ and later, Enterprise Edition.
SQL Server read replicas use native SQL Server Always On technology under the hood.
Multi-AZ for SQL Server uses Always On Availability Groups (SQL Server 2017+) or Database Mirroring (older versions).
RDS supports storage auto-scaling: automatically increases storage when usage exceeds threshold.
Maximum storage: 64 TiB for most engines, 16 TiB for SQL Server.
pricing_summary: Instance hours (varies by instance class and engine) + storage (gp2: $0.115/GB/month, gp3: $0.08/GB/month + provisioned IOPS/throughput). SQL Server has license costs included in instance pricing. Multi-AZ doubles instance and storage costs.
common_misconceptions: RDS SQL Server DOES have managed read replicas. This was added in 2022 and is commonly overlooked.
gp2 is NOT always slower than gp3. For volumes under 1TB, gp2 can burst to 3,000 IOPS. For steady-state workloads, gp3 provides 3,000 baseline IOPS at lower cost.
You do NOT need Provisioned IOPS (io1/io2) for 3,000 IOPS. Both gp2 (via burst) and gp3 (baseline) provide this.
RDS storage auto-scaling does NOT require manual intervention. It automatically increases storage when free space drops below 10%.

one_liner: DynamoDB includes built-in export-to-S3, point-in-time recovery, Streams, and global tables with no custom code required.
when_to_use: Need to export DynamoDB table data to S3 for analytics (use native export, not custom Lambda pipeline)
Need continuous backups with point-in-time recovery (PITR) to any second in the last 35 days
Need change data capture for downstream processing (DynamoDB Streams)
when_not_to_use: Need real-time streaming to non-AWS destinations (consider Kinesis Data Streams with DynamoDB integration)
Need complex SQL joins across tables (use a relational database)
Export needs to be real-time continuous (Streams + Lambda for near-real-time; native export is a point-in-time batch)
key_facts: Native Export to S3: zero-code, zero-capacity export of full table data to S3 in DynamoDB JSON or Amazon Ion format. Does NOT consume read capacity. Uses PITR backup as source.
Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR): continuous backups with restore to any second in the last 35 days. Restores to a new table.
DynamoDB Streams: ordered, time-series log of every item-level change (insert, update, delete). Integrates with Lambda, Kinesis Data Streams, and Kinesis Client Library.
Global Tables: multi-region, multi-active replication with sub-second replication latency. All regions accept writes.
TTL: automatically deletes expired items at no cost. Deletion is eventually consistent (items may persist up to 48 hours past TTL).
On-demand backup: full table backup that can be retained indefinitely. Restores to a new table.
Import from S3: load data from S3 into a new DynamoDB table without writing custom code.
pricing_summary: Export to S3: $0.11 per GB exported. PITR: $0.20 per GB per month. Streams: no additional charge for DynamoDB Streams reads; Lambda triggers have standard Lambda pricing. Global Tables: replicated write capacity is charged in each replicated region.
common_misconceptions: DynamoDB HAS native export to S3. You do NOT need to build a Lambda scan-and-write pipeline. The native export uses PITR and does not consume table read capacity.
PITR restores to a NEW table, not in-place. Plan for table rename or application pointer update after restore.

DynamoDB Streams is NOT the same as Kinesis Data Streams. Streams is built into DynamoDB with 24-hour retention. Kinesis integration is an alternative with longer retention and broader consumer support.
Global Tables replication is NOT instant. Sub-second typical, but conflict resolution is last-writer-wins.
